flashcards.front: What is the primary purpose of software metrics?
flashcards.back: To provide quantitative measures for assessing software development processes, product attributes, and project outcomes.
QA focuses on preventing defects through process improvement, while QC identifies defects through product inspection/testing.
Lines of Code measures program size and is used for estimating effort/maintenance costs.
M = E - N + 2P where E=edges, N=nodes, P=connected components in control flow graph.
Program complexity through operators/operands counts, calculating volume, difficulty, and effort.

flashcards.front: Name three factors in McCall's quality model.
flashcards.back: Product Operation: Correctness, Reliability, Efficiency. Product Revision: Maintainability, Flexibility. Product Transition: Portability.
International standard defining 6 main quality characteristics: functionality, reliability, usability, efficiency, maintainability, portability.
To detect defects early, share knowledge, and ensure adherence to coding standards.
Defined processes: organization has standardized processes documented and institutionalized.
Static code analysis tool for continuous inspection of code quality and technical debt management.
Number of defects per size unit (e.g., defects/KLOC) indicating software quality.
80% of codebase executed during testing, helping assess test suite effectiveness.
